description: Build the full shopping list management experience in the native Swift app — list CRUD, sharing, realtime sync, custom emoji grid picker, and navigation to list detail view.

userStories.title: Codable Data Models for Lists & Shares
userStories.description: Create Swift data models that map to the Supabase lists and list_shares tables, with CodingKeys for snake_case ↔ camelCase conversion.
acceptanceCriteria: A GatherList model with properties: id (UUID), ownerId (UUID), name (String), emoji (String?), itemCount (Int), color (String), createdAt (Date). Conforms to Codable and Identifiable.
A ListShare model with properties: id (UUID), listId (UUID), sharedWithEmail (String), addedAt (Date). Conforms to Codable and Identifiable.
Column name mapping uses CodingKeys to convert between snake_case DB columns and camelCase Swift properties (e.g., owner_id ↔ ownerId, item_count ↔ itemCount, shared_with_email ↔ sharedWithEmail).
Default values match database defaults: itemCount = 0, color = '#1565c0', emoji = nil.
Models compile and are usable with the Supabase Swift SDK's .select() and .insert() queries.
xcodebuild build succeeds
userStories.notes: GatherList and ListShare Codable models with CodingKeys for snake_case mapping. Build succeeds.
userStories.testIntensityReason: Data structure definitions — compile-time verification only

userStories.title: ListService — CRUD Operations
userStories.description: Create a service layer that wraps Supabase queries for list and share operations including fetch, create, update, delete, share, and unshare.
acceptanceCriteria: A ListService class (or actor) provides async methods: fetchOwnedLists(userId:), createList(userId:name:emoji:color:), updateList(listId:updates:), deleteList(listId:), shareList(listId:email:), unshareList(listId:email:), fetchSharesForList(listId:), fetchSharedWithMe(email:)
fetchOwnedLists fetches lists where owner_id = userId, ordered by created_at ascending
createList inserts a new list and returns it
shareList inserts a list_shares row with normalized (lowercased, trimmed) email
fetchSharedWithMe fetches list_shares rows for the user's email, then fetches the parent lists to return merged data including list name and owner ID
All methods use the Supabase Swift SDK's PostgREST API
Errors are thrown with descriptive context (e.g., 'Failed to create list: name=Groceries')
The service does NOT manually manage item_count — that's handled by database triggers
xcodebuild build succeeds
userStories.notes: ListService with static async methods: fetchOwnedLists, createList, updateList, deleteList, shareList, unshareList, fetchSharesForList, fetchSharedWithMe. PostgREST queries, error context, no manual item_count management.
userStories.testIntensityReason: Core data operations with multiple query patterns

userStories.title: ListViewModel — State Management with @Observable
userStories.description: Create the main observable view model that manages all list state (owned + shared), active list selection, search filtering, and realtime subscriptions.
acceptanceCriteria: A ListViewModel using @Observable manages: ownedLists, sharedLists, activeListId, isLoading, error, searchQuery
A computed property allLists merges owned + shared lists (owned first, then shared)
A computed property filteredLists filters allLists by searchQuery against list name and emoji
On initialization, fetches owned lists and shared list references, auto-selects the first list as active
Realtime subscriptions: lists table filtered by owner_id, list_shares table filtered by shared_with_email, individual shared list metadata
On any realtime change event, re-fetches the entire dataset (re-fetch-all pattern matching React)
Exposes action methods: createList(name:emoji:color:), updateList(id:name:emoji:color:), deleteList(id:), selectList(id:)
deleteList only works for lists the user owns; after deletion, auto-selects the next available list
Realtime channels are properly cleaned up when the view model is deinitialized
xcodebuild build succeeds
userStories.notes: @Observable ListViewModel with owned+shared list state, allLists/filteredLists computed properties, parallel data loading, dual realtime subscriptions (lists+list_shares), re-fetch-all pattern, CRUD actions, ownership-guarded delete, channel cleanup on deinit.
userStories.testIntensityReason: State management with realtime subscriptions and channel lifecycle

userStories.title: List Browser View — Owned & Shared Sections
userStories.description: Build the main list browser UI with owned and shared sections, color accents, search bar, loading state, and empty state.
acceptanceCriteria: The Lists tab shows a scrollable list separated into 'My Lists' (owned) and 'Shared with me' sections
The 'Shared with me' section header only appears when there are shared lists
Each list row displays: emoji (or default icon), list name, item count badge, and the list's color as a 4px left-edge accent
Shared lists show a 'Shared' badge (blue tint) to distinguish them from owned lists
Tapping a list row navigates to the list detail view (items are Phase 3, but navigation must work with empty state)
A search bar at the top filters lists by name and emoji as the user types
The view uses the ListViewModel for all data and actions
Loading state shows a spinner while initial data loads
Empty state shows a friendly message when the user has no lists with a create button
The view supports both light and dark mode
xcodebuild build succeeds
userStories.notes: ListBrowserView with My Lists/Shared sections, ListRowView with 4px color accent + emoji + item count badge + Shared badge, searchable filter, loading/empty states, NavigationLink to placeholder detail, Color(hex:) extension. Replaced ListsPlaceholderView in MainTabView.
userStories.testIntensityReason: Primary list UI with multiple sections, search, and navigation

userStories.title: Create List Flow — Name, Emoji & Color Picker
userStories.description: Implement the create-list bottom sheet with name input, custom emoji grid picker with categories and search, and 10-color preset picker.
acceptanceCriteria: A '+' button in the list browser's toolbar triggers the create flow
The create flow is presented as a bottom sheet (.sheet modifier), sliding up like iOS Reminders' New List
The sheet contains: text field for list name, emoji picker button, and a row of 10 color preset circles
The 10 preset colors match the React app: #1565c0, #2e7d32, #c62828, #ef6c00, #6a1b9a, #00838f, #ad1457, #f9a825, #37474f, #4e342e
The selected color circle has a visible checkmark or border indicator; default is #1565c0
The emoji picker is a custom grid picker — scrollable grid organized by category (Food & Drink, Animals, Objects, Smileys, etc.) with search bar at top, presented as nested sheet or navigation push
Tapping 'Create' calls ListViewModel.createList and auto-selects the new list; sheet dismisses automatically
The name field is required — Create button is disabled when name is empty
The sheet has Cancel and Create buttons in a top navigation bar area
xcodebuild build succeeds
userStories.notes: CreateListSheet bottom sheet with name field, emoji picker button, 10 preset color circles with checkmark selection. Custom EmojiPickerView with 7 categories, LazyVGrid search, and clear option. Toolbar + button in ListBrowserView.
userStories.testIntensityReason: User input form with custom emoji grid picker and color selection

userStories.title: List Context Menu — Edit & Delete
userStories.description: Add contextual actions for each list row: edit name/emoji/color via bottom sheet and delete with confirmation dialog.
acceptanceCriteria: Each list row has a context menu with options based on ownership: owned lists get 'Name & Icon', 'Share Settings', 'Delete List'; shared lists get no edit/delete options
Name & Icon opens an edit bottom sheet (same style as create) for changing name, emoji, and color (same 10 presets + custom emoji grid picker)
Saving the edit calls ListViewModel.updateList(id:name:emoji:color:)
Delete List shows a confirmation dialog with list name and warning about permanent deletion
Confirming delete calls ListViewModel.deleteList(id:)
If the deleted list was the active list, the view auto-navigates to the next available list
The context menu feels native to iOS (use .contextMenu, .swipeActions, or Menu as appropriate)
xcodebuild build succeeds
userStories.notes: Context menu on owned list rows with Name & Icon, Share Settings (placeholder), Delete List. EditListSheet bottom sheet reusing emoji picker and color presets. Delete confirmation alert. Swipe-to-delete action. Emoji clearing fix in ListViewModel.
userStories.testIntensityReason: Destructive actions with confirmation and ownership-based permissions

userStories.title: Share List Modal — Add & Remove Collaborators
userStories.description: Implement list sharing by email with collaborator display, add/remove functionality, and basic validation.
acceptanceCriteria: Share Settings from the context menu opens a share view/sheet showing list name, email input, Add button, and current collaborators
Adding a collaborator calls ListService.shareList with email lowercased and trimmed
Each collaborator row shows their email and a Remove button (trash icon or swipe-to-remove)
Removing a collaborator calls ListService.unshareList
The collaborator list updates in realtime via the list_shares subscription from US-003
Basic validation: prevent empty email, show error if already shared (UNIQUE constraint), prevent sharing with owner's own email
Only list owners can access Share Settings — option does not appear for shared lists
xcodebuild build succeeds
userStories.notes: ShareListSheet with email input, validation (empty, format, self-share, duplicate), collaborator list with trash button and swipe-to-delete. ListViewModel shareList/unshareList methods. Share Settings wired in context menu.
userStories.testIntensityReason: Cross-user sharing logic with email validation and realtime updates

userStories.title: List Detail View — Navigation & Empty State
userStories.description: Create the list detail view with navigation from browser, share status indicator, empty state for items, and toolbar placeholder for Phase 3.
acceptanceCriteria: Tapping a list in the browser pushes a detail view using NavigationStack / NavigationLink
The detail view's navigation title shows the list name (with emoji if set)
The detail view shows the list's color as an accent (e.g., nav bar tint or header element)
Share status indicator: if shared, display collaborator count (e.g., person icon with badge count) for owned lists, or 'Shared by [owner]' for lists shared with the user; no indicator if not shared
When the list has no items, display a centered empty state: icon + 'No items yet' + 'Add items to get started' subtitle
Back button or swipe-back gesture returns to the list browser (default NavigationStack behavior)
The detail view receives the list ID and resolves metadata from ListViewModel
A toolbar area is prepared for the Add Item input that Phase 3 will build
The view supports both light and dark mode
xcodebuild build succeeds
userStories.notes: ListDetailView with colored navigation bar, share status indicator (collaborator count for owned, 'Shared' badge for shared), empty state, items count placeholder, and add item toolbar placeholder. Replaced ListDetailPlaceholderView in navigation destination.
userStories.testIntensityReason: Navigation shell with empty state and share indicator

userStories.title: Realtime Sync — Live Updates Across Devices
userStories.description: Verify and polish realtime behavior so changes made on the React web app or another device appear instantly in the Swift app.
acceptanceCriteria: When a list is created/renamed/deleted on the React web app, the Swift app's list browser updates within a few seconds without manual refresh
When a list is shared with the current user (on web or another device), it appears in the 'Shared with me' section automatically
When a share is removed, the list disappears from the shared section automatically
When item_count changes (items added/removed on web), the badge updates in the list browser
Pull-to-refresh is available as a manual fallback in the list browser
No duplicate realtime channels are created when navigating between tabs or backgrounding/foregrounding the app
Realtime channels reconnect after network interruption (verify Supabase SDK handles this)
xcodebuild build succeeds
userStories.notes: Realtime subscriptions verified (owned lists + shared lists channels). Duplicate channel guard added. Public refresh() method for pull-to-refresh. .refreshable modifier on ListBrowserView. SDK handles reconnection automatically. Build succeeds.
userStories.testIntensityReason: Cross-device data consistency with multiple realtime channels and reconnection behavior
